titlestmt.titleproper: Koch, Georgia E.; Course Notebook ua802.015
titlestmt.author: Gregory Wiedeman

publicationstmt.publisher: M.E. Grenander Department of Special Collections & Archives

repository.corpname: M.E. Grenander Department of Special Collections & Archives

did.physloc: The materials are located onsite in the department.
did.abstract: A 1926 Home Economics notebook documenting the education and early teaching career of New York State College for Teachers alumna Georgia E. Koch.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Collection consists primarily of a Home Economics notebook of Georgia E. Koch, a student at the New York State College for Teachers.
The notebook contains handwritten notes, diagrams, and pasted instructional material dating to June–August 1926, reflecting coursework or summer-session professional study in Home Economics.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Georgia Elizabeth Koch attended the New York State College for Teachers after graduating from Schenectady High School and pursued a degree in Home Economics.
While a student, she was active in campus organizations including the Consumer's League, Newman Club, Music Club, Spanish Club, and the Girls' Athletic Association.
She was elected to Omicron Nu, the national honor society for Home Economics, and was also a member of the Eta Phi literary society.
Koch graduated in 1922 and subsequently worked as a Home Economics teacher for approximately five years.
She married Verne V. Hemstreet in 1927 after meeting him in Schenectady,

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The order of the pages was maintained as provided by the donor.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Donated by Catherine S. Hyman, granddaughter of Georgia E. Koch in 2026.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to this collection is unrestricted.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The researcher assumes full responsibility for conforming to the laws of copyright.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Preferred citation for this material is as follows: "Identification of specific item, series, box, folder, Georgia E. Koch Course Notebook, 1926. M.E. Grenander Department of Special Collections and Archives, University Libraries, University at Albany, State University of New York (hereafter referred to as the Koch Notebook)."
